chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الصَّدَقَةِ عَلَى أَهْلِ الْبَوَادِي وَ أَهْلِ السَّوَادِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 57 – The charity upon the people of the valleys and the people of the wilderness

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول ويحتمل الضعف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/141)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول ويحتمل الضعف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/141)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) إِنَّ أَهْلَ السَّوَادِ يَقْتَحِمُونَ عَلَيْنَا وَ فِيهِمُ الْيَهُودُ وَ النَّصَارَى وَ الْمَجُوسُ فَنَتَصَدَّقُ عَلَيْهِمْ فَقَالَ نَعَمْ .

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Ali Bin Al Hakam Bin Miskeen, from Amro Bin Abu Nasr who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘The people of the wilderness are swooping upon us, and among them are the Jews, and the Christians, and the Magians, so can we give charity to them?’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes’.
en.sarwar: 3. Ahmad ibn Muhammad has narrated from Muhammad ibn Ali from al-Hakam ibn Miskin from ‘Amr ibn abu Nasr who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, ‘People who live in wilderness come to us. Among them are Jews, Christian and Zoroastrians. Can we give them charity?’ The Imam replied, ‘Yes, you
